title: A Theoretical Framework for the Critical Posthumanities
author: Braidotti

opening_move.description: The convergence of posthumanism and post-anthropocentrism in the contemporary moment is producing new transdisciplinary fields, but lacks a rigorous theoretical foundation. The paper proposes a neo-Spinozist materialist ontology as that foundation for the 'critical posthumanities'.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Without a proper theoretical foundation, the posthumanities risk being co-opted by cognitive capitalism and transhumanist projects (Bostrom); a materialist ontology of radical immanence is the ethical alternative.

rivals_engaged: transhumanism / Oxford Future of Humanity Institute (Bostrom)
theoretical_positioning.description: The paper positions 'critical posthumanities' against the hegemonic transhumanist model (Bostrom) and against humanist universalism, proposing a neo-materialist, zoe-centred, affirmative alternative grounded in Deleuzian ontology.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Philosophical argument and cartographic synthesis: mapping the field of posthumanist theory and constructing a normative-ontological framework for the critical posthumanities.
epistemic_mode.notes: The paper is an extended programmatic argument — a manifesto-like theoretical framework. No empirical material. Extensively footnoted to signal intellectual community rather than as evidence.

confidence_notes: This is a programmatic theoretical essay — close to a manifesto for the critical posthumanities as a field. The normative stake is high and explicit: cognitive capitalism and transhumanism are the enemy; nomadic minor science and affirmative posthuman ethics are the alternative. No case is used. The move sequence is essentially: diagnose the problem → map the field → propose a framework → derive implications for university knowledge production.
